verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass type I TM protein with its N-terminal EF-hand/SAM domain in the ER lumen and C-terminus in the cytosol; its dominant home is the ER membrane. However, STIM1 has a well-documented PM-resident pool (often termed STIM1L or surface STIM1) in several cell types, and ER-PM junctional clustering during store-operated calcium entry brings the ER-luminal ectodomain to within nanometers of the PM but not through it. More importantly, surface STIM1 has been detected by surface biotinylation and flow cytometry on platelets and some other cells with the EF-hand/SAM domain exposed extracellularly, and anti-STIM1 antibodies targeting the ectodomain have been described. This qualifies as dual_localization — minority PM pool alongside dominant ER residency. Not yes because ER is the canonical compartment.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Uncertain how robust the surface STIM1 pool is across tissues vs. being an ER-resident protein only juxtaposed to PM at ER-PM junctions.
